LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE, AND A REQUEST FOR AN ORDER ENTERING ALL REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION DEEMED ADMITTED. THE MATTER IS CALLED. NO ARGUMENT IS HELD. THE COURT ADOPTED THE TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: THE JUDGE PRO TEM'S REPORT AND RECOMMENDATIONS ARE ADOPTED, AND DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE GROUP'S DISCOVERY MOTION IS THUS GRANTED AND DENIED AS FOLLOWS: MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO FORM INTERROGATORIES 2.2-2.7, 7.1, 12.1, 12.7 AND 17.1 AND REQUESTS FOR ADMISSION 7 AND 9-10 IS GRANTED; THIS MOTION IS OTHERWISE DENIED. MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES 16, 19, 32-34, 37, 40, 43, 46 AND 49 IS GRANTED. ALL FURTHER RESPONSES SHALL BE CODE-COMPLAINT AND ARE DUE BY JUNE 11, 2021. MOTION TO HAVE ADMISSIONS ADMITTED IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: RICHARD B. ULMER, JR.; CLERK: JACQUELINE ALAMEDA; COURT REPORTER: ROSE MILLER, CSR NO. 5041, EMAIL: RMILLERCSR@GMAIL.COM. (302/RBU)

Notice In Support Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One, And A Request For An Order Entering All Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar May-21-2021 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances (via ZOOM videoconference):
Atty. Jaime Y. Ritton, Esq. of Coddington Hicks & Danforth, 650.592.5400, for Defendant CSAA Insurance Exchange.
No appearance by any other party or counsel.

DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One, And A Request For An Order Entering All Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted.

The matter is called. NO ARGUMENT is held. Ms. Ritton states that she is appearing in case there is anything that the Court wants her to address. At the conclusion of Ms. Ritton's comments, the Court adopted the tentative ruling as follows:

The judge pro tem's report and recommendations are adopted, and defendant CSAA Insurance Group's discovery motion is thus granted and denied as follows:

Motion to compel further responses to form interrogatories 2.2-2.7, 7.1, 12.1, 12.7 and 17.1 and requests for admission 7 and 9-10 is granted; this motion is otherwise denied.

Motion to compel further responses to special interrogatories 16, 19, 32-34, 37, 40, 43, 46 and 49 is granted.

All further responses shall be code-complaint and are due by June 11, 2021.

Motion to have admissions admitted is denied.

Order signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer, Jr.; Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Rose Miller, CSR No. 5041, Email: RMillerCSR@gmail.com. (302/RBU)

Notice In Support Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One, And A Request For An Order Entering All Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted
Off Calendar Apr-30-2021 Continued To May-21-21 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Edward Kerley (510-379-5801) of Kerley Schaffer LLP for Plaintiff.
Jaime Ritton (650-592-5400) of Coddington, Hicks & Danforth for Defendant.

Plaintiff's Counsel does not stipulate to the matter being heard by the Judge Pro Tem.

ARGUED. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows:

DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANGE's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, Set One And Special Interrogatories, Set One, And A Request For An Order Entering All Requests For Admission Deemed Admitted:
The matter is continued to May 21, 2021 at 9:30 am in Department 302, Law and Motion Department.
Judge Pro Tem to prepare report and recommendation.

Judge Pro Tem: Katherine Gallo, Clerk: S. Le, Not reported. =(302/JPT)
